title: Cambridge CAE 3 — Test 4 (Reading & Use of English)

sections.title: Part 1: Multiple Choice (Reading)
sections.instructions: You are going to read three extracts which are all concerned in some way with journalism. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text.
sections.passageHtml:
Extract 1: How can I get into music journalism?

If you want to become a music journalist, there are several starting points. One is to have a good knowledge of the greats, because when you write you have to put today’s music into context. It isn’t enough simply to be familiar with the music of the last five years. Another way is to specialise in a particular type of music, such as folk, country, techno or ambient. And the third starting point is to be a musician. This opens doors to the magazines targeted specifically at musicians rather than a more general readership. There may be opportunities if you don’t fit any of those three categories, but it’s a competitive marketplace so it’s important that in some way you meet the sometimes quite specific needs of the music magazines.

Staff jobs do get advertised from time to time, but your best approach might be to work freelance. This way you can do non-music writing as well, if necessary, to add to your income, and equally importantly, you’ll have greater creative freedom by writing for different music magazines. In general they make sure they meet their readers’ very specific expectations, and so you may find writing just for one is limiting.

Review of Journalism: A Very Short Introduction

by Ian Hargreaves

This book was published before under a different title and the material has been reissued in the A Very Short Introduction ... series of titles which aim to give readers a brief, colourful insight into a subject and ignite a passion for further learning and discovery. The series is an alternative to the Introducing ... series of yesteryear, which briefly introduced readers to people, topics and ideas. Journalism: A Very Short Introduction suffers where the Introducing... titles excelled. Humour was used by the Introducing ... series to great effect to draw the reader in and make the subject covered that much more interesting. So much rests on how a subject is taught, and being likened to a textbook, which is a real risk in the present case, would be the kiss of death for a title such as this.

This very short introduction to journalism is bang up to date. Hargreaves wants journalists to question the function they serve and for the public to question journalists. Never has this been more important. Media networks regularly appeal for eyewitness accounts and video from the scenes of breaking news, snippets of the action sent direct to the news studio via mobile phones. We are all becoming journalists. So, where does that leave the journalists? This book does not, in the end, explain what makes a journalist tick. It is rather an instruction manual, and readers are very likely to learn something of interest from it.

A closer look at newspaper editorials

Simply put, a newspaper editorial is an article that expresses an opinion. While this single sentence may not seem to do justice to defining what an editorial is, think about this brief definition. What other types of article express this defining characteristic? What other articles actively promote the expression of an opinion? Reviewers may include factual information, so their personal opinions do not totally dominate a review, and news reporters attempt to give ‘just the facts’ about a situation. Editorials are one of the few types of articles that celebrate the expression of opinion; they are wholly centred on the opinion being presented by the writer and the debate and discussion that the opinion incites.

That said, an editorial writer often attempts to the best of his or her own ability to defend the opinion that is being presented against potential counterarguments — a good editorial does not appear in a vacuum; this defence employs argument, logic, and other appeals to readers in an attempt to achieve a desired effect. The purpose of any given editorial is entirely directed by its writer. Most often, however, editorial writers seek to prove the validity of an opinion through argument, point out something that has been ignored, or simply create discussion about a topic. In sum, most editorials will inevitably involve the defence of an opinion in the hopes of advancing some goal that the writer has in mind.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the correct letter A, B, C or D.

questions.instruction: 1. What does the writer say about becoming a music journalist?
questions.explanation: Chọn B — 'Opportunities are mostly limited to certain categories of people.' (Các cơ hội hầu hết bị giới hạn trong một số nhóm người cụ thể). Trong đoạn 1, tác giả nêu ra ba điểm khởi đầu cụ thể để bước chân vào nghề báo chí âm nhạc: (1) hiểu biết sâu sắc về các danh ca/tượng đài âm nhạc cổ điển ('knowledge of the greats'), (2) chuyên sâu vào một thể loại nhạc nhất định ('specialise in a particular type of music'), và (3) bản thân phải là nhạc sĩ ('be a musician'). Tác giả nhấn mạnh: 'There may be opportunities if you don’t fit any of those three categories, but it’s a competitive marketplace so it’s important that in some way you meet the sometimes quite specific needs of the music magazines.' Điều này cho thấy cơ hội chủ yếu chỉ mở ra cho các nhóm đối tượng cụ thể đáp ứng được các nhu cầu khắt khe này. ❌ A sai vì tác giả chỉ khuyên nên chuyên sâu một thể loại cụ thể chứ không nói những người thành công nhất phải tìm lĩnh vực ít người làm. ❌ C sai vì không có thông tin nào cho rằng người viết có phong cách cá nhân độc đáo thì dễ vào nghề nhất. ❌ D sai vì công việc cơ hữu/biên chế vẫn được tuyển dụng thỉnh thoảng ('Staff jobs do get advertised from time to time') chứ không phải đã hoàn toàn biến mất hoặc cực kỳ hiếm hoi. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 48 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: B. Opportunities are mostly limited to certain categories of people. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. The most successful journalists find a field that few people specialise in.
B. Opportunities are mostly limited to certain categories of people.
C. It is easiest for someone who writes with an individual style.
D. Traditional opportunities for journalists are now uncommon.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 2. In the second paragraph the writer says that each music magazine tends to
questions.explanation: Chọn A — 'publish pieces that fit within a relatively narrow range.' (xuất bản các bài viết nằm trong phạm vi tương đối hẹp). Trong đoạn 2, tác giả viết: 'In general they [music magazines] make sure they meet their readers’ very specific expectations, and so you may find writing just for one is limiting.' (Nhìn chung, các tạp chí luôn cố gắng đáp ứng kỳ vọng rất cụ thể của độc giả của họ, và do đó bạn sẽ thấy việc chỉ viết cho một tạp chí là rất hạn chế). Điều này ngụ ý rằng nội dung của mỗi tạp chí bị định hình bởi tệp độc giả trung thành và có phạm vi rất hẹp, khiến nhà báo tự do không phát huy hết khả năng sáng tạo nếu chỉ cộng tác với một nơi. ❌ B sai vì mặc dù độc giả có mong đợi cụ thể, bài viết không nói tạp chí khuyến khích nhà báo đi tìm hiểu mong đợi đó. ❌ C sai vì tạp chí đi theo đáp ứng mong đợi của độc giả chứ không có ý định định hướng hay thay đổi, phát triển thị hiếu mới của họ. ❌ D sai vì tạp chí đi theo hướng chuyên biệt và đáp ứng mong đợi cụ thể chứ không khuyến khích viết đa dạng các chủ đề. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 48 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: A. publish pieces that fit within a relatively narrow range. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. publish pieces that fit within a relatively narrow range.
B. encourage writers to find out what readers expect.
C. publish pieces that will develop their readers’ tastes.
D. encourage writers to deal with a variety of topics.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 3. The writer compares two series of books to suggest that Journalism: A Very Short Introduction is not sufficiently
questions.explanation: Chọn D — 'entertaining for its readers.' (mang tính giải trí đối với người đọc). Tác giả so sánh loạt sách mới này với loạt sách 'Introducing...' trước đây. Loạt sách cũ đã xuất sắc ('excelled') vì sử dụng 'Humour... to great effect to draw the reader in and make the subject covered that much more interesting' (sử dụng khiếu hài hước rất hiệu quả để thu hút độc giả và làm chủ đề thú vị hơn nhiều). Trái lại, cuốn sách mới của Hargreaves lại quá khô khan và bị ví như một cuốn sách giáo khoa ('textbook'), điều này bị coi là 'kiss of death' (nụ hôn thần chết - sự thất bại hoàn toàn) cho một tác phẩm thuộc thể loại này. Điều này chứng tỏ tác phẩm mới thiếu đi sự hài hước và tính giải trí cần có. ❌ A sai vì sách vẫn cung cấp thông tin hữu ích và độc giả dễ dàng học được điều gì đó thú vị ('learn something of interest'). ❌ B sai vì tác giả chỉ trích phong cách khô khan chứ không nói nội dung thiếu tính độc bản/nguyên gốc. ❌ C sai vì sách bị chê là quá giống sách giáo khoa (quá hàn lâm) chứ không phải thiếu tính hàn lâm. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 49 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: D. entertaining for its readers. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 4. According to the second paragraph, the book explores
questions.explanation: Chọn A — 'the role of journalists in modern news coverage.' (vai trò của các nhà báo trong việc đưa tin thời hiện đại). Trong đoạn 2, tác giả viết: 'Hargreaves wants journalists to question the function they serve and for the public to question journalists... We are all becoming journalists. So, where does that leave the journalists?' Điều này có nghĩa là cuốn sách khám phá và thảo luận về vai trò, chức năng của các nhà báo chuyên nghiệp trong thời đại kỹ thuật số, khi mà bất cứ ai cũng có thể quay phim, chụp ảnh hiện trường gửi cho đài truyền hình để làm tin tức. ❌ B sai vì tác giả ghi rõ cuốn sách 'does not, in the end, explain what makes a journalist tick' (không giải thích động cơ hay điều gì thôi thúc một người làm nhà báo). ❌ C sai vì cuốn sách kêu gọi công chúng đặt câu hỏi về nhà báo chứ không tập trung phân tích thái độ của người bình thường đối với báo chí. ❌ D sai vì sách không liệt kê các cơ hội việc làm đa dạng trong ngành. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 49 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: A. the role of journalists in modern news coverage. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. the role of journalists in modern news coverage.
B. the motivation of people who become journalists.
C. the attitudes of ordinary people towards journalism.
D. the variety of jobs available to journalists.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 5. In the first paragraph the writer is
questions.explanation: Chọn D — 'differentiating between various types of newspaper writing.' (phân biệt giữa các thể loại viết báo khác nhau). Trong đoạn đầu, tác giả chỉ ra sự khác biệt rõ rệt giữa bài xã luận và các thể loại khác: bài phê bình (reviews - có chứa thông tin thực tế bên cạnh ý kiến), bài đưa tin (news reports - cố gắng đưa tin khách quan 'just the facts'), và khẳng định xã luận là loại bài viết duy nhất tôn vinh việc thể hiện ý kiến cá nhân và kích thích tranh luận ('wholly centred on the opinion... debate and discussion'). ❌ A sai vì tác giả không so sánh giữa các tờ báo khác nhau mà so sánh các thể loại bài viết. ❌ B sai vì tác giả không khuyên đọc hay đề xuất một nhóm nhà báo cụ thể nào. ❌ C sai vì tác giả đang định nghĩa và phân loại bài xã luận chứ không nhấn mạnh việc đọc nhiều quan điểm khác nhau. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 49 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: D. differentiating between various types of newspaper writing. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. showing how one newspaper differs from another.
B. recommending a particular category of newspaper writers.
C. stressing the importance of reading different opinions.
D. differentiating between various types of newspaper writing.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 6. According to the second paragraph, editorial writers are likely to
questions.explanation: Chọn C — 'take other people’s opinions into account.' (cân nhắc đến ý kiến của người khác). Trong đoạn 2, tác giả viết: '...an editorial writer often attempts... to defend the opinion that is being presented against potential counterarguments — a good editorial does not appear in a vacuum; this defence employs argument, logic...' (một người viết xã luận thường cố gắng bảo vệ quan điểm trước những lập luận phản bác tiềm ẩn - một bài xã luận tốt không xuất hiện trong chân không; sự phòng thủ này sử dụng lập luận, logic...). Để có thể bảo vệ ý kiến của mình trước lập luận phản bác (counterarguments) từ người khác, người viết xã luận buộc phải xem xét và suy nghĩ về những quan điểm trái chiều đó trước. ❌ A sai vì họ bảo vệ quan điểm chứ không giải thích khi nào họ sẽ đổi ý. ❌ B sai vì bài viết nói họ bảo vệ quan điểm của mình chứ không bảo họ giả vờ có ý kiến khác thực tế. ❌ D sai vì họ dùng lập luận phản bác chứ không phải đi tóm tắt ý kiến của người viết khác về cùng chủ đề. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 49 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: C. take other people’s opinions into account. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. explain the circumstances in which they could change their opinion.
B. claim to have opinions that they do not really hold.
C. take other people’s opinions into account.
D. summarise other writers’ opinions on the same topic.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple Choice (Reading)

sections.title: Part 2: Gapped Text (Reading)
sections.instructions: You are going to read an extract from a magazine article about Cape Cod. Six paragraphs have been removed from the extract. Choose from the paragraphs A–G the one which fits each gap (7–12). There is one extra paragraph which you do not need to use.
sections.passageHtml:
Where the landscape will do the walking

Despite the growth of tourism in the area, Roger Bray finds there are still undeveloped parts of Cape Cod, an exposed peninsula off the east coast of the USA.

On the fragile outer shore of Cape Cod the pervading sense is of a universe in which nothing stands still. The ocean wages its war of attrition against the shifting sand, which rises from the beach into a steep cliff. Gulls wheel on the wind, swallows dart low over the water's edge.

[ Gap 7 ]

The simple reason is that, here, more than in most places, to get off the roads and away from the most easily accessible beaches is to experience the Cape not just as a holiday retreat for urban Americans but as it has always been.

[ Gap 8 ]

This is mainly because a large swathe of it was established in 1961 as a national park. Our search for recommended hikes took us to the internet — but the maps were hard to follow. We tried bookshops but to no avail. There were books listing walks, to be sure, but the routes they covered were much too short.

[ Gap 9 ]

Following its directions made for superb hiking. To cover the whole of the route we wanted to do would have involved linear sections totalling about 50 kilometres. There were circular itineraries, however, varying in length between about 12 and 20 kilometres, though slow going on soft sand makes them seem longer.

[ Gap 10 ]

One route took us along the Old King’s Highway, once a stagecoach route, into the middle of an eerie swamp of Atlantic white cedar, where the sunlight streamed between shaggy barked trunks and where the park management has built a boardwalk and provided nature information.

[ Gap 11 ]

The circuit concluded with an intoxicating hike along the beach. To our right rose the huge sandy cliff, threatening to slide and bury the unwary. Henry Beston, in The Outermost House, his lyrical account of a year spent here in the 1920s, describes how, after the cliff was pushed back six metres or so by a momentous storm, the long buried wreckage of ships emerged from it, as fruit from a sliced pudding.

[ Gap 12 ]

The shingled Whalewalk Inn was also a delight. It lies behind a white painted picket fence on a leafy road on the fringe of Eastham. It was built in 1830 by Henry Harding, a whaling captain when that industry was at its peak. Later it was used as a farmhouse and a salt works. Nowadays, people also find it a relaxing place to stay.

Paragraphs options (A–G):

A. It continued to the South Wellfleet sea cliff where Marconi broadcast the first transatlantic wireless message in 1903. The transmitting station was scrapped in 1920 but a model recalls how it looked, its antennae suspended between tall timber masts.

B. If we had sauntered a few kilometres from the car park to stand for a while on that great beach, we might still have felt the whirling of the universe. But without a day of serious hiking to sharpen our appetites, would we have appreciated the food so much?

C. On the other side, however, there was nothing but ocean, jade green inshore, ink blue farther out, between us and the coast of north-west Spain. Although this was a week of near flawless weather in May, we were lucky to encounter only a handful of other walkers. In high summer, when the roads are clogged and there are queues for restaurant tables, it is harder to find an empty stretch of beach.

D. Because, for all the impact of tourism, which nearly triples the population in summer, there are still lonely parts of this storm-scoured, glacial peninsula which have changed little during the last 150 years.

E. We tried several of them. Sometimes we were on woodland trails shaded partly by pitch pine and black oak, sometimes on high windy cliffs overlooking the sea, and sometimes on the foreshore, where we were made diminutive by the huge sky and curving beach of white gold sand.

F. Henry David Thoreau wrote that ‘even the sedentary man here enjoys a breadth of view which is almost equivalent to motion’. Perhaps that was why it proved so difficult to find a guide for long hikes. People must wonder why they need to expend effort when they can let the landscape do the walking.

G. Staff at the inquiry desk of the Cape Cod National Seashore’s Salt Pond visitor centre were no help, either. But in the centre’s bookshop, we struck gold at last. Adam Gamble’s In the Footsteps of Thoreau, published locally two years ago, has a section tracing the writer's progress in 1849 from Eastham to Race Point Beach, where he turned towards Provincetown, the Cape's outermost community, now a gathering place for whale watchers.

questionGroups.instruction: Select the correct paragraph (A–G) for each gap.

questions.instruction: Gap 7:
questions.explanation: Chọn F. Đoạn trước mô tả chuyển động của sóng và gió ở bờ biển ('Gulls wheel on the wind...'). Đoạn F bắt đầu bằng việc dẫn lời của Henry David Thoreau về chuyển động và góc nhìn ở Cape Cod ('...almost equivalent to motion'). Cuối đoạn F kết luận: 'People must wonder why they need to expend effort when they can let the landscape do the walking' (Mọi người chắc hẳn tự hỏi tại sao phải tốn sức khi họ có thể để phong cảnh tự đi bộ hộ mình). Ý này kết nối chặt chẽ với câu đầu đoạn sau Gap 7: 'The simple reason is that, here, more than in most places, to get off the roads... is to experience the Cape... as it has always been.' (Lý do đơn giản là ở đây, để ra khỏi các con đường và đi bộ là để trải nghiệm...). Bẫy: Các đoạn khác không chứa thông tin liên kết trực tiếp về đi bộ/chuyển động của cảnh quan. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 50 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: F. Answer-key option. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 2: Gapped Text (Reading)

questions.instruction: Gap 8:
questions.explanation: Chọn D. Đoạn trước mô tả mục đích đi bộ là để trải nghiệm bán đảo như thuở ban sơ ('experience the Cape... as it has always been'). Đoạn D mở đầu bằng liên từ 'Because' giải thích cho câu trên: 'Because, for all the impact of tourism... there are still lonely parts of this storm-scoured, glacial peninsula which have changed little during the last 150 years' (Bởi vì, bất chấp tác động của du lịch, vẫn có những phần đất cô lập ít thay đổi suốt 150 năm). Câu sau Gap 8 tiếp nối bằng nguyên nhân hành chính: 'This is mainly because a large swathe of it was established in 1961 as a national park' (Điều này chủ yếu là nhờ phần lớn diện tích đã được thiết lập thành công viên quốc gia từ năm 1961). Sự kết hợp giữa 'changed little' và 'national park' là vô cùng logic. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 50 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: D. Answer-key option. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 2: Gapped Text (Reading)

questions.instruction: Gap 9:
questions.explanation: Chọn G. Đoạn trước kể về việc tìm kiếm bản đồ đi bộ trên mạng và hiệu sách nhưng thất bại: 'We tried bookshops but to no avail. There were books listing walks, to be sure, but the routes they covered were much too short.' Đoạn G tiếp nối việc tìm kiếm không thành công tại quầy thông tin: 'Staff at the inquiry desk... were no help, either.' Tuy nhiên, họ đã tìm thấy sách của Adam Gamble ở cửa hàng lưu niệm của trung tâm. Đoạn sau tiếp tục: 'Following its directions made for superb hiking' (Đi theo chỉ dẫn của cuốn sách này mang lại một chuyến đi tuyệt vời). Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 50 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: G. Answer-key option. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 2: Gapped Text (Reading)

questions.instruction: Gap 10:
questions.explanation: Chọn E. Đoạn trước nhắc đến các hành trình đi bộ vòng tròn khác nhau ('circular itineraries'). Đoạn E tiếp nối ngay bằng đại từ 'them' thay thế cho các hành trình này: 'We tried several of them. Sometimes we were on woodland trails...'. Đoạn tiếp theo mô tả cụ thể một trong những cung đường đã thử: 'One route took us along the Old King’s Highway...' Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 50 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. questions.instruction: Gap 11:
questions.explanation: Chọn A. Đoạn trước đang mô tả một cung đường cụ thể kết thúc ở khu vực có biển thông tin tự nhiên ('provided nature information'). Đoạn A tiếp nối cung đường này: 'It continued to the South Wellfleet sea cliff where Marconi broadcast...'. Câu sau Gap 11 kết luận về toàn bộ hành trình: 'The circuit concluded with an intoxicating hike along the beach' (Vòng lặp kết thúc bằng một chuyến đi dọc bãi biển). Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 50 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 2: Gapped Text (Reading)

questions.instruction: Gap 12:
questions.explanation: Chọn C. Đoạn trước mô tả không gian bên phải của người đi bộ là vách cát lớn ('To our right rose the huge sandy cliff...'). Đoạn C tiếp nối bằng cách mô tả phía đối diện: 'On the other side, however, there was nothing but ocean...' (Tuy nhiên ở phía bên kia, không có gì ngoài đại dương). Đây là sự liên kết không gian vô cùng chuẩn xác ('To our right...' vs 'On the other side...'). Đoạn sau chuyển sang kể về nhà nghỉ Whalewalk Inn. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 50 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. sections.title: Part 3: Multiple Choice (Reading)
sections.instructions: You are going to read a magazine article about an artist. For questions 13–19, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text.
sections.passageHtml:
Under Sarah's Spell

Sarah Janson is a trompe-l’oeil artist whose paintings are designed to deceive the eye by creating the illusion of reality. Here she is interviewed by Joanna Watt.

There cannot be many artists who do not sign their work unless they are asked to. Sarah Janson, a trompe-l’oeil artist, is one. She is not remotely interested in the concept of the artist as creator, let alone that of the artist as genius: ‘It’s not the artist who is important, but the work,’ she states. Janson is so self-deprecating that she would almost like you to believe that her trompe-l’oeil works paint themselves.

All of which does not bode well for a magazine interview. ‘I just don’t like to shout about myself,’ she says, and then covers her face in horror when asked if she minds being photographed for the feature. Cut to her sitting room 30 minutes later (a wonderful space in a block of artists’ studios in London, filled with paintings and drawings) and you find two women bent double with hysteria. Her confidence gained, the interview becomes a fascinating, amusing (and sometimes hilarious) encounter.

Janson has been a trompe-l’oeil artist for sixteen years, after two years’ solid drawing at art school (‘the best training any artist can ever have’), a degree in graphic illustration and a stint at a publishing house. But illustration never really satisfied her, and she joined a specialist decorator, Jim Smart (‘one of the best in his day’). Smart asked her to do one trompe-l’oeil, and that was it. ‘Suddenly my interest got channelled,’ she says. She left to set up on her own, ‘not really knowing where I was going, but feeling that I was on the road to somewhere.’ Her instinct was right.

Janson’s observational skills and fascination with detail (gained through illustrating) proved essential qualities for a trompe-l’oeil artist. ‘People often ask me where they can learn trompe-l’oeil. But no one can teach you. Trompe-l’oeil is the school of life. It’s all about observation.’ She insists (in that self-deprecating way) that she is still learning. ‘The moment you think that you've mastered a field you might as well give up.’ She is also brutally honest about her ‘failings’ (‘I can't paint bread; it always looks like grey concrete’) and is frank about her mathematical abilities. Faced with a huge commission for the domed chapel ceiling at Lulworth Castle, she became totally confused when calculating measurements. ‘I thought to myself, “You’re not Michelangelo. Who do you think you are?” This habit of self-questioning and a reluctance to openly acknowledge her skill has spawned an oddly distanced attitude to her talent. Janson often speaks in the third person: ‘When I finished that ceiling, I thought, “Well I didn’t do it, she did”.’

Of course, her trompe-l'oeil schemes can speak for themselves. Janson’s work is in a league of its own, far above those who have jumped on the bandwagon (the art of trompe-l’oeil has experienced something of a revival, but not with entirely satisfactory results) and she has a string of major corporate and private commissions behind her. Much of her work is inspired by architecture or made for architectural settings. There is the trompe-l’oeil dining room for one client, based on the façade of a Venetian palazzo, and the painting at the end of a corridor in a flat, which gives the illusion that you can step into two further rooms.

There is always a danger with trompe-l’oeil, though, that once you get the joke, your attention is lost, something of which Janson is acutely aware. ‘Trompe-l’oeil has to do two things. First, it must draw you in; it’s got to trick you. Secondly, it has to hold you and then engage your imagination. That is the most important part.’

While trompe-l’oeil has to be clever, it must also, Janson believes, be personal to the client. ‘I love the interaction with clients; that is where the ideas are born,’ she says. ‘Without the rapport, the job of creating a trompe-l’oeil scheme becomes rather difficult. Some clients have firm ideas about what they want; others do not. You have to be willing to listen. You have to get inside a client’s imagination.’ Many have become friends, not least because Janson practically lives with them if she works on site.

Janson is generous in praise of her clients. ‘I am very grateful for the mad ones who have let me loose on their walls,’ she confesses. And, they too, seem delighted with her, which is why she is constantly busy — despite her inclination to play down her talent. ‘I really don’t like to shout about myself,’ she repeats at the end. ‘Like my work, I am very restrained. I don’t want it to shout. You become bored with things that shout.’ True, perhaps, but you could never really become bored with Janson or her work. It certainly deserves to become better known, and I am prepared to incur her wrath while I blow her trumpet.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the correct letter A, B, C or D.

questions.instruction: 13. After spending time with Janson, the interviewer concludes that
questions.explanation: Chọn C — 'her initial doubts about Janson were wrong.' (những nghi ngờ ban đầu của cô ấy về Janson là sai). Trong đoạn 2, người phỏng vấn viết: 'All of which does not bode well for a magazine interview.' (Tất cả những điều đó không báo hiệu điềm tốt cho một cuộc phỏng vấn trên tạp chí) do tính cách quá khiêm tốn và rụt rè của Sarah. Tuy nhiên, chỉ 30 phút sau, họ đã cùng cười hả hê và cuộc phỏng vấn trở thành 'a fascinating, amusing (and sometimes hilarious) encounter' (một cuộc gặp gỡ hấp dẫn, vui vẻ và đôi khi rất vui nhộn). Điều này chứng minh sự e ngại ban đầu của người phỏng vấn đã sai. ❌ A sai vì không có thông tin Sarah thiếu tin tưởng vào giới báo chí. ❌ B sai vì Sarah vui vẻ tiếp đón và cười đùa thoải mái tại nhà/phòng làm việc của cô sau đó chứ không phải ghét phỏng vấn tại nhà. ❌ D sai vì người phỏng vấn không hề đưa ra câu hỏi mang tính đe dọa hay áp đặt nào. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 51 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: C. her initial doubts about Janson were wrong. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. Janson has little faith in journalists.
B. Janson dislikes interviews in her home.
C. her initial doubts about Janson were wrong.
D. her first questions to Janson were threatening.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 3: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 14. What motivated Janson to start her own business as a trompe-l'oeil artist?
questions.explanation: Chọn C — 'She was convinced it was what she wanted to do.' (Cô ấy tin chắc đó là điều mình muốn làm). Trong đoạn 3, Janson cho biết việc minh họa sách không làm cô thỏa mãn. Sau khi được người hướng dẫn Jim Smart yêu cầu vẽ thử một bức tranh trompe-l'oeil, sở thích của cô đã được định hướng hoàn toàn. Cô quyết định ra làm riêng: 'not really knowing where I was going, but feeling that I was on the road to somewhere. Her instinct was right.' (không thực sự biết mình sẽ đi đâu, nhưng cảm thấy mình đang trên đường tới một nơi nào đó. Trực giác của cô đã đúng). Điều này cho thấy động lực của cô xuất phát từ niềm tin mãnh liệt vào việc mình muốn làm. ❌ A sai vì mặc dù cô có năng khiếu vẽ vững chắc, nhưng trình độ không phải là động lực thúc đẩy cô lập nghiệp. ❌ B sai vì cô không ghét bỏ hay bất mãn với chủ cũ (Jim Smart được cô ca ngợi là 'one of the best in his day'). ❌ D sai vì cô đi theo trực giác chứ không lập kế hoạch rõ ràng để đạt được tham vọng lớn lao nào. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 51 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: C. She was convinced it was what she wanted to do. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. It was something she was well qualified to do.
B. She was unhappy with her previous employer.
C. She was convinced it was what she wanted to do.
D. It was something that would help her achieve her ambitions.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 3: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 15. What advice does Janson give to people interested in becoming trompe-l’oeil artists?
questions.explanation: Chọn A — 'It would be a mistake to become over-confident.' (Sẽ là một sai lầm nếu trở nên quá tự tin). Trong đoạn 4, Janson chia sẻ: 'The moment you think that you've mastered a field you might as well give up.' (Khoảnh khắc bạn nghĩ rằng mình đã thành thạo một lĩnh vực thì bạn cũng nên từ bỏ nó đi). Điều này ngụ ý nghệ sĩ phải luôn khiêm tốn, tiếp tục học hỏi và không được tự mãn hay quá tự tin. ❌ B sai vì cô nói vẽ bánh mì của cô vẫn giống 'bê tông xám' chứ không khuyên luyện tập là cách duy nhất khắc phục điểm yếu. ❌ C sai vì cô cho rằng nghệ thuật này cần quan sát thực tế cuộc sống chứ không khuyên trải nghiệm các hình thức nghệ thuật khác nhau. ❌ D sai vì cô nhấn mạnh trường học cuộc sống và quan sát thực tế ('Trompe-l’oeil is the school of life. It’s all about observation') chứ không nói về việc nhìn vào tác phẩm của nghệ sĩ khác. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 51 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: A. It would be a mistake to become over-confident. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. It would be a mistake to become over-confident.
B. Practice is the only way to improve shortcomings.
C. Experience in different art forms helps develop essential skills.
D. A lot can be gained from looking at the work of other artists.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 3: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 16. What point does the interviewer make about Janson’s work in the fifth paragraph?
questions.explanation: Chọn A — 'It is of an exceptional quality.' (Nó có chất lượng xuất sắc/đặc biệt). Trong đoạn 5, người phỏng vấn viết: 'Janson’s work is in a league of its own, far above those who have jumped on the bandwagon...' Cụm từ 'in a league of its own' (ở một đẳng cấp riêng biệt, vượt trội hoàn toàn so với những người khác) chứng tỏ chất lượng tác phẩm của cô là cực kỳ xuất sắc và độc đáo. ❌ B sai vì tác phẩm được khen ngợi hết lời và có nhiều đơn đặt hàng lớn chứ không bị coi là kỳ quái. ❌ C sai vì tác phẩm của cô được ứng dụng ở nhiều không gian lớn như lâu đài Lulworth Castle và phòng ăn Venetian Palazzo chứ không chỉ hợp với không gian nhỏ. ❌ D sai vì không có thông tin nói cô hối tiếc về các dự án đã nhận. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 51 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: A. It is of an exceptional quality. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. It is of an exceptional quality.
B. Some people regard it as strange.
C. It is better suited to small locations.
D. Janson regrets some of the commissions she has taken on.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 3: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 17. What does Janson say about trompe-l’oeil as an art form?
questions.explanation: Chọn D — 'The difficulty lies in sustaining people’s interest.' (Khó khăn nằm ở việc duy trì sự hứng thú của người xem). Trong đoạn 6, tác giả viết: 'There is always a danger with trompe-l’oeil, though, that once you get the joke, your attention is lost, something of which Janson is acutely aware.' (Tuy nhiên, luôn có mối nguy hiểm với vẽ đánh lừa thị giác là một khi bạn nhận ra trò lừa, sự chú ý của bạn sẽ mất đi). Do đó, cô kết luận tác phẩm thành công ngoài việc đánh lừa lúc đầu thì quan trọng nhất phải giữ chân và lôi cuốn được trí tưởng tượng của họ lâu dài. ❌ A sai vì cô có rất nhiều hợp đồng thương mại lớn từ các tập đoàn và cá nhân. ❌ B sai vì thể loại này bản chất chứa đựng yếu tố đùa giỡn, đánh lừa ('get the joke') chứ không phải tránh sự hài hước. ❌ C sai vì không đề cập đến số lượng người coi đây là nghệ thuật thực sự. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 51 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: D. The difficulty lies in sustaining people’s interest. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. It has limited commercial appeal.
B. The most successful pieces avoid humour.
C. A small number of people accept it as genuine art.
D. The difficulty lies in sustaining people’s interest.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 3: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 18. What does Janson say about her clients?
questions.explanation: Chọn D — 'A commission is easier if you can discover what kind of ideas the client has.' (Dự án sẽ dễ dàng hơn nếu bạn có thể khám phá xem khách hàng có những ý tưởng gì). Trong đoạn 7, cô nói: 'Without the rapport, the job of creating a trompe-l’oeil scheme becomes rather difficult... You have to get inside a client’s imagination.' (Nếu không có sự thấu hiểu/mối quan hệ tốt, công việc tạo ra tác phẩm trở nên khá khó khăn... Bạn phải thâm nhập được vào trí tưởng tượng của khách hàng). Điều này đồng nghĩa việc khám phá ra ý tưởng của khách hàng giúp công việc thuận lợi và dễ dàng hơn. ❌ A sai vì cô phục vụ cả khách hàng có ý tưởng rõ ràng lẫn những người không có ý tưởng cụ thể ('Some clients have firm ideas... others do not'). ❌ B sai vì cô không chê ý tưởng của khách hàng là kém thực tế. ❌ C sai vì cô nói phải sẵn sàng lắng nghe khách hàng chứ không bảo cô sẽ từ chối nếu không đồng ý với họ. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 51 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: D. A commission is easier if you can discover what kind of ideas the client has. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. She prefers to work with clients who have a lot of imagination.
B. Some clients have ideas which are less practical than others.
C. She is reluctant to take on commissions if she cannot agree with the client.
D. A commission is easier if you can discover what kind of ideas the client has.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 3: Multiple Choice (Reading)

questions.instruction: 19. What does the interviewer say in the final paragraph?
questions.explanation: Chọn B — 'She realises that Janson may not like what she has written about her.' (Cô ấy nhận ra Janson có thể không thích những gì cô viết về mình). Ở cuối bài viết, người phỏng vấn cho biết: '...and I am prepared to incur her wrath while I blow her trumpet.' (Tôi sẵn sàng gánh chịu cơn thịnh nộ của cô ấy trong khi tôi ca ngợi cô ấy). Vì Sarah là người rất kín đáo, không thích ồn ào và tự quảng cáo bản thân ('restrained', 'don’t like to shout about myself'), bài viết phỏng vấn ca tụng này có thể sẽ khiến Sarah không thích và tức giận ('incur her wrath'). ❌ A sai vì người phỏng vấn bày tỏ lòng biết ơn đối với những khách hàng 'điên rồ' của Sarah chứ không hề tỏ ra bối rối. ❌ C sai vì tác phẩm của Sarah rất bền vững và khó nhàm chán chứ không nhanh mất sức hút. ❌ D sai vì người phỏng vấn cho rằng nghệ thuật này xứng đáng được biết đến nhiều hơn chứ không phải là không thể phổ biến hơn. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 51 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: B. She realises that Janson may not like what she has written about her. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
options: A. She is puzzled by the way Janson describes her clients.
B. She realises that Janson may not like what she has written about her.
C. She is sure that certain types of art soon lose their appeal.
D. She feels that trompe-l’oeil is unlikely to become a more popular art form.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 3: Multiple Choice (Reading)

sections.title: Part 4: Multiple Matching (Reading)
sections.instructions: You are going to read an article from a book about being a shopkeeper. For questions 20–34, choose from the sections (A–E). The sections may be chosen more than once.
sections.passageHtml:
HOW TO RUN A SHOP

Ian Maitland offers a practical survival guide to retailing today.

Section A

Shopkeeping, whether in a bakery or toystore, differs greatly from how people on the other side of the counter fondly imagine it to be. In theory, there are many positive aspects: you are your own boss and are able to choose which hours, days and weeks to work; it is an almost idyllic existence with little to do except sit behind a counter each day reading a book with one hand whilst raking in money with the other; all you need to do is smile sweetly at customers as you take their money and you'll be sitting on a goldmine. In reality, there are innumerable negative aspects which people do not always recognise. Whatever the trade, the hours are long — far lengthier than most employees ever have to work. Shopkeeping is physically demanding as well, in most trades. Even worse, shopkeeping is mentally stressful. You have to be forever vigilant to keep costs down and profits up, buying and selling at the right prices, paying invoices and chasing payments at the correct times. Through all of this you need to keep smiling, staying cheerful for your customers. Don’t forget you’re at the sharp end too: suppliers, employees and customers with problems will come to you, expecting their difficulties to be solved immediately.

Section B

Shopkeeping is above all financially hazardous, especially in recessionary times. Although statistics vary, it is undeniable that most small, independent shops close down within a few years of opening their doors. Some retailers find themselves unsuited to the lifestyle, or perhaps they make too many errors, such as insufficient funding, a poorly chosen business, too much slow-selling stock, inadequate control systems and so on. Others do everything properly but find circumstances are against them; perhaps a nationally known store opens nearby, or a recession takes hold of the market. Do not be under any illusions: even if your approach is perfect, the unforgiving statistics suggest you are as likely to fail as to succeed - and soon!

Section C

If you are determined to press ahead with becoming a shopkeeper, first consider all the qualities required to be a success and whether or not you possess them in abundance. You need a mix of self-confidence and willpower to get a business off the ground and keep it going year after year. Your temperament is important: losing your temper with stonewalling bank managers, slow-to-deliver suppliers and confused customers will be counterproductive. Suddenly deciding to carry a new range of goods, hold a sale or drop an existing service without fully appraising the likely pros and cons of different courses of action is a recipe for impending setbacks or out-and-out disaster.

Section D

Some knowledge and experience of your proposed trade, and retailing in general, are essential if you intend to start a business from scratch and very desirable if you are going to purchase an existing concern. The complete novice who has never worked in the chosen trade nor stood behind a counter in his or her life has almost no chance of success. There is much to learn in so little time, and the pitfalls are many and varied. The winning retailer has to be a good all-rounder, able enough to spot a suitable site and quality premises, charm a bank manager, suppliers and customers, keep accurate books and records, advertise his or her business and so on. Too often, retailers have a single predominant skill to the exclusion of others. They may have first-class products but do not possess the social skills required to sell them, or buy popular goods from wholesalers but under- or overprice or mistreat them. You ought to be a jack of all trades — and a master of them all too.

Section E

Think about your motives for going into the retail trade, to make sure that they are truly appropriate. Ideally, you should almost burn inside to be your own boss, knowing you have what it takes — temperament, skills and so forth — to make a go of your down-to-earth ideas. It should be a lifelong ambition. A brooding sense of feeling unfulfilled in a job, the fear or reality of unemployment, a surplus of redundancy money to spend, a desire to do ‘something’: all these are inappropriate reasons which smack of uncertainty and half-heartedness. These are not factors that should be driving you into shopkeeping. Then consider your goals, being certain that they are wholly realistic. Earning a reasonable living, equal to or slightly above the average industrial wage, for a hard day’s graft is achievable. ‘Having a great time’, ‘taking things easy’, ‘having a bit of a laugh’ and ‘making a fortune’ — all of which are real quotes from would-be retailers — reveal attitudes that are likely to lead to abject failure, and deservedly so.

sections.title: Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze
sections.instructions: For questions 1–12, read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).
sections.passageHtml:
We really can tell if we are being watched

Stories about how people somehow know when they are being watched have been going around for years. However, few (0) attempts have been made to investigate the phenomenon scientifically. Now, with the completion of the largest ever study of the so-called staring effect, there is impressive evidence that this is a recognisable and (1) ..... sixth sense.

The study (2) ..... hundreds of children. For the experiments, they sat with their eyes covered and with their backs to other children, who were told to either stare at them or look away. The results consistently showed that the children who could not see were able to (3) ..... when they were being stared at. In a total of 18,000 trials (4) ..... worldwide, the children (5) ..... sensed when they were being watched almost 70% of the time.

The experiment was repeated with the (6) ..... precaution of putting the children who were being watched outside the room, (7) ..... from the starers by the windows. This was done just in case there was some (8) ..... going on, with the children telling each other whether they were looking or not. This (9) ..... the possibility of sounds being (10) ..... between the children. The results, though less impressive, were more or less the same. Dr Sheldrake, the biologist who designed the study, believes that the results are (11) ..... enough to find out through further experiments (12) ..... how the staring effect might actually come about.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the correct letter A, B, C or D.

questions.instruction: Gap (1):
questions.explanation: Chọn A — 'genuine'. Cụm 'genuine sixth sense' nghĩa là 'giác quan thứ sáu thực thụ'. Từ 'genuine' (tính từ) nghĩa là thật, đích thực, không giả tạo. ❌ B 'accepted' (được thừa nhận rộng rãi) không phù hợp vì hiện tượng này vẫn đang trong quá trình điều tra khoa học xem có tồn tại hay không. ❌ C 'carefully' (cẩn thận) và D 'precisely' (chính xác) là các trạng từ, không thể đứng trước cụm danh từ 'sixth sense' để bổ nghĩa như tính từ. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 54 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context. Language rule: Part 1 tests lexical choice, collocation, phrasal verbs and register; the correct option must fit both meaning and pattern.
questions.answerLocation: A. genuine. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze

questions.instruction: Gap (2):
questions.explanation: Chọn A — 'involved'. 'The study involved hundreds of children' nghĩa là 'nghiên cứu bao gồm/có sự tham gia của hàng trăm trẻ em'. 'Involve' có nghĩa là bao gồm, lôi cuốn ai đó tham gia vào. ❌ B 'contained' (chứa đựng, kiểm soát) và D 'enclosed' (đính kèm, gửi kèm trong phong bì) không dùng để chỉ sự tham gia của con người trong nghiên cứu. ❌ C 'comprised' (bao gồm, gồm có) thường cấu trúc trực tiếp 'comprised hundreds of children' nhưng trong tiếng Anh học thuật, khi nói về đối tượng tham gia dự án, thử nghiệm thì động từ 'involved' là phổ biến và tự nhiên hơn cả. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 54 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: A. involved. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze

questions.instruction: Gap (3):
questions.explanation: Chọn C — 'tell'. Cấu trúc 'be able to tell when...' nghĩa là 'có thể nhận biết/phân biệt/biết được khi nào...'. Đây là nét nghĩa đặc biệt của động từ 'tell' (nhận biết, phân biệt, nói rõ sự khác biệt). ❌ A 'find' (tìm kiếm), B 'notice' (chú ý - thường đòi hỏi các giác quan thông thường như nhìn hay nghe thấy trực tiếp), D 'reveal' (tiết lộ) đều không đi với mệnh đề 'when they were being stared at' theo nghĩa 'cảm nhận/nhận biết được' như trong bài. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 54 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: C. tell. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.

questions.instruction: Gap (4):
questions.explanation: Chọn D — 'carried out'. Phrasal verb 'carry out' nghĩa là tiến hành, thực hiện (nghiên cứu, thí nghiệm, thử nghiệm). 'trials carried out worldwide' = những thử nghiệm được tiến hành trên khắp thế giới. ❌ A 'worked over' (nghiên cứu lại kỹ lưỡng, hoặc đánh đập dã man), B 'worked through' (giải quyết lần lượt từ đầu đến cuối một vấn đề phức tạp), C 'carried on' (tiếp tục hành động) đều không collocate với danh từ 'trials' trong ngữ cảnh này. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 54 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: D. carried out. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.

questions.instruction: Gap (5):
questions.explanation: Chọn A — 'correctly'. Trạng từ 'correctly' (một cách chính xác, đúng đắn) đi với động từ 'sensed' để diễn đạt việc trẻ em cảm nhận đúng (trúng) khi nào chúng bị nhìn chằm. ❌ B 'exactly' (chính xác về số liệu, thời gian cụ thể), C 'thoroughly' (hoàn toàn, kỹ lưỡng từ đầu đến cuối), D 'perfectly' (một cách hoàn hảo) không phù hợp để chỉ việc đoán trúng kết quả của một thí nghiệm nhị phân (đúng/sai). Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 54 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context. Language rule: Part 1 tests lexical choice, collocation, phrasal verbs and register; the correct option must fit both meaning and pattern.
questions.answerLocation: A. correctly. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze

questions.instruction: Gap (6):
questions.explanation: Chọn B — 'added'. Cụm danh từ 'added precaution' (biện pháp phòng ngừa bổ sung) là một collocation cố định dùng phổ biến để chỉ việc thiết lập thêm một quy tắc an toàn nhằm tăng độ tin cậy. ❌ A 'attached' (được đính kèm), C 'connected' (được kết nối), D 'increased' (được tăng lên) không tạo thành cụm từ đi cùng tự nhiên với danh từ 'precaution' để chỉ một quy trình thí nghiệm mới thêm vào. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 54 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: B. added. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze

questions.instruction: Gap (7):
questions.explanation: Chọn A — 'separated'. Cấu trúc bị động 'be separated from someone/something' nghĩa là 'bị ngăn cách/chia tách khỏi ai/cái gì'. Trong bài, trẻ bị theo dõi được đặt bên ngoài phòng, ngăn cách với người nhìn chằm qua lớp kính cửa sổ. ❌ B 'parted' (thường dùng cho người chia tay, rời xa nhau về mặt tình cảm hoặc đi hai hướng khác nhau), C 'split' (chia tách một thực thể thống nhất ra làm hai hoặc nhiều mảnh, chia rẽ nội bộ), D 'divided' (chia nhỏ số lượng thành các phần) đều không collocate thích hợp trong việc ngăn cách không gian vật lý ở thí nghiệm này. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 54 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: A. separated. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze

questions.instruction: Gap (8):
questions.explanation: Chọn C — 'cheating'. Từ 'cheating' (gian lận) ở danh từ dạng V-ing trong cấu trúc 'there was some cheating going on' để chỉ hành vi gian lận (báo hiệu cho nhau xem có đang nhìn hay không). ❌ A 'pretending' (giả vờ), B 'lying' (nói dối), D 'deceiving' (lừa dối, lừa gạt - thường mang tính chất lớn hơn và không đi với cấu trúc danh từ hóa không đếm được 'some deceiving going on' một cách tự nhiên như 'cheating'). Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 54 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: C. cheating. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze

questions.instruction: Gap (9):
questions.explanation: Chọn A — 'prevented'. Động từ 'prevented' ở quá khứ đơn phù hợp ngữ cảnh của câu: 'This prevented the possibility of...' (Điều này đã ngăn chặn khả năng của...). Cấu trúc: 'prevent something' hoặc 'prevent someone from doing something'. ❌ B 'omitted' (lược bỏ, bỏ sót), C 'evaded' (né tránh nghĩa vụ, lẩn tránh câu hỏi), D 'ended' (kết thúc) không thể đi với tân ngữ 'the possibility' theo nét nghĩa ngăn ngừa rủi ro/khả năng xảy ra âm thanh này. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 54 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: A. prevented. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze

questions.explanation: Chọn C — 'transmitted'. Động từ 'transmit' (truyền dẫn) dùng cho việc truyền tín hiệu, âm thanh, ánh sáng, nhiệt hoặc thông tin thông qua một phương tiện vật lý (như qua tường, cửa kính). 'sounds being transmitted between the children' = âm thanh bị truyền dẫn giữa những đứa trẻ. ❌ A 'delivered' (giao hàng, phát biểu bài viết), B 'transported' (vận chuyển bằng xe cộ), D 'distributed' (phân phát, phân phối) không dùng cho sự lan truyền sóng âm vật lý. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 54 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context. Language rule: Part 1 tests lexical choice, collocation, phrasal verbs and register; the correct option must fit both meaning and pattern.
questions.answerLocation: C. transmitted. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze

questions.explanation: Chọn B — 'convincing'. Tính từ 'convincing' (thuyết phục, đáng tin cậy) bổ nghĩa cho kết quả nghiên cứu: 'results are convincing enough to...' (kết quả đủ sức thuyết phục để...). Sau cấu trúc 'be + adj + enough'. ❌ A 'satisfying' (đáp ứng được yêu cầu, đem lại sự hài lòng) không mang nghĩa thuyết phục về mặt học thuật/khoa học. C 'concluding' (mang tính kết luận - kết quả thí nghiệm chưa thể khẳng định chắc chắn 100% cơ chế nên không thể dùng 'concluding' mà chỉ là đủ thuyết phục để nghiên cứu thêm), D 'persuading' (dạng V-ing hoạt động như tính từ bổ nghĩa cho người chứ kết quả nghiên cứu phải dùng 'convincing'). Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 54 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context.
questions.answerLocation: B. convincing. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze

questions.explanation: Chọn D — 'precisely'. Trạng từ 'precisely' (chính xác, tường tận) đứng trước từ hỏi 'how' ('precisely how the staring effect...') để nhấn mạnh mục tiêu nghiên cứu tiếp theo là tìm hiểu chính xác bằng cách nào hiện tượng này thực sự diễn ra. ❌ A 'really' (thực sự), B 'carefully' (một cách cẩn thận), C 'definitely' (chắc chắn) không mang nghĩa nhấn mạnh tính khoa học, chi tiết và chính xác về mặt cơ chế vận hành như 'precisely'. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 54 Method: read both sides of the gap and choose the option that fits meaning, collocation and grammar. Trap: reject distractors that are close in meaning but do not fit the pattern or register in context. Language rule: Part 1 tests lexical choice, collocation, phrasal verbs and register; the correct option must fit both meaning and pattern.
questions.answerLocation: D. precisely. Evidence cue: use this answer-key option with the passage/audio paraphrase; confirm the full claim, not just a repeated keyword.

sections.title: Part 2: Open Cloze
sections.instructions: For questions 13–27, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).
sections.passageHtml:
The toughest runners

There are a few runners who have completed every London Marathon (0) since the first race in 1981. They are the toughest runners of (13) ..... . These athletes, (14) ..... honour of both their mental and physical strength, have been given a permanent entry in the event for the rest of their lives, provided that they do not miss a year. Other people have run the race faster or under greater handicaps, (15) ..... these are athletes with a mission. For (16) ....., the annual event is a way of life, not just a worthy fund-raising exercise (17) ..... a single challenge. Bill O’Connor is one of these runners. In (18) ..... case, running is a daily ritual which began in New Zealand (19) ....., as a youngster, he pounded along the wet sand on the edge of the Tasman Sea.

Now aged fifty, (20) ..... working as a mathematics teacher at a school in London, he retains his fascination (21) ..... the London Marathon and the activity of running. He says, ‘When the first London Marathon was held, | thought (22) ..... myself that here was a challenge. | thought that if there was only going to be one race, | wanted to have run in (23) ..... .’ But the London Marathon went (24) ..... to become the most impressive success story in British sport and Bill O’Connor has been a constant part of it. (25) ..... he ever felt that he would fail to finish? ‘In 1985. It was a beautiful day and | started running much (26) ..... fast for the first mile and got worried. So | slowed down for the next mile. Yet (27) ..... | expected | would take at least four hours, | finished in two hours thirty-four minutes and twenty-nine seconds.’ It is his best time so far.

acceptedAnswers: They are the toughest runners of all
questions.explanation: Điền 'ALL'. Cụm từ 'of all' đứng sau dạng so sánh nhất hoặc tính từ chỉ tính chất cực hạn để nhấn mạnh: 'toughest runners of all' (những người chạy kiên cường nhất trong số tất cả mọi người). Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 55 Method: complete the gap with "ALL" only after checking the words before and after it. sections.title: Part 3: Word Formation
sections.instructions: For questions 28–37, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).
sections.passageHtml:
Gulangyu

One of the most (0) delightful places in China is a small island called Gulangyu. It is often called ‘piano island’ because of the large number of (28) ..... who live there. But perhaps Gulangyu’s greatest appeal is that although it has 20,000 permanent (29) ....., mostly living in a central community one kilometre wide, there are no high-rise buildings and motor vehicles and bicycles are banned. Personal (30) ..... are carried, or pushed on small wagons.

While Gulangyu is a popular day trip for people from the neighbouring island of Xiamen, it seems a relatively (31) ..... destination for visitors from elsewhere in China or further afield. One reason why Gulangyu has not been besieged by Chinese visitors is its (32) ..... location. Several mountain ranges on mainland China mean that road and rail (33) ..... from the prosperous cities to its east and south are very slow. Air travel, via Xiamen airport, is a possibility, but it is too expensive for the majority of visitors.

There are certainly enough attractions for those tourists who do arrive, including parks, an aquarium and souvenir shops. There are also some excellent restaurants, though they may charge (34) ..... more than those found in other, less (35) ..... parts of the country. However, one key tourist business that is not much in (36) ..... is hotels — there are only a (37) ..... in the centre of the island.

questions.instruction: Gap (28) [MUSIC]:

⚠️ Dạng bài riêng biệt của kỳ thi CAE cũ

Mỗi câu hỏi dưới đây bao gồm 3 câu riêng biệt có khoảng trống. Bạn phải tìm MỘT từ tiếng Anh duy nhất có thể điền vào cả 3 khoảng trống một cách hợp ngữ pháp và hợp nghĩa. Từ cần tìm có thể thay đổi dạng từ (tính từ, danh từ, động từ) hoặc là thành phần của các collocations/idioms khác nhau trong mỗi câu.

questionGroups.instruction: Write one word which fits all three sentences.

questions.instruction:
Q38.
• Once the management has decided on the __________ of events for the training day, a letter will be sent to all staff.
• The President gave the __________ for a holiday to be declared to celebrate the birth of his son.
• I'd asked them to deliver the fridge but it never arrived, and when I enquired the company said they had no trace of my __________.
acceptedAnswers: hợp lệ, 'out of order' hỏng hóc, 'place an order' đặt hàng.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: ORDER (danh từ: thứ tự, mệnh lệnh, đơn hàng). • Câu 1: 'order of events' = trình tự/thứ tự diễn ra của các sự kiện. • Câu 2: 'give the order for something' = ban lệnh, ra lệnh thực hiện việc gì. • Câu 3: 'no trace of my order' = không tìm thấy dấu vết/thông tin nào về đơn đặt hàng của tôi. 💡 Mẹo: 'Order' là một danh từ cực kỳ đa năng trong tiếng Anh công sở và đời sống. Hãy lưu ý các cụm: 'in order' (đúng thứ tự/hợp lệ), 'out of order' (hỏng hóc), 'place an order' (đặt hàng). Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 56 Method: complete the gap with "ORDER" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure. Language rule: key word transformation keeps the key word unchanged while preserving meaning through grammar pattern and lexical control.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write one word which fits all three sentences. Q38. • Once the management has decided on the __________ of events for.... Correct completion: ORDER.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 4: Gapped Sentences

questions.instruction:
Q39.
• After months of intensive training, Glyn thought he was in with a __________ chance of winning a medal at the games.
• The manager made a __________ argument for lowering the price of the company’s product.
• The __________ currents in the bay make sailing tricky even in perfect weather conditions.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: STRONG (tính từ: lớn/cao, đanh thép/sắc bén, mạnh). • Câu 1: 'in with a strong chance of V-ing' = có cơ hội lớn/khả năng cao sẽ làm được gì. • Câu 2: 'a strong argument' = một lập luận đanh thép, đầy sức thuyết phục. • Câu 3: 'strong currents' = những dòng hải lưu mạnh/dòng nước xiết. 💡 Mẹo: Tính từ 'Strong' không chỉ dùng cho thể chất (strong man) mà còn kết hợp rộng rãi trong tiếng Anh học thuật để chỉ các lập luận, cơ hội hoặc hiện tượng tự nhiên. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 56 Method: complete the gap with "STRONG" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure. Language rule: key word transformation keeps the key word unchanged while preserving meaning through grammar pattern and lexical control.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write one word which fits all three sentences. Q39. • After months of intensive training, Glyn thought he was in with.... Correct completion: STRONG.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 4: Gapped Sentences

questions.instruction:
Q40.
• They had a long wait at the airport so they __________ the time doing puzzles.
• Dan __________ the contract to me so that I could check its accuracy.
• The car that __________ us on the motorway was going far too fast.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: PASSED (động từ ở dạng quá khứ của PASS: tiêu khiển, chuyển giao, vượt qua). • Câu 1: 'pass the time' = giết thời gian, tiêu khiển thời gian trong lúc chờ đợi. • Câu 2: 'pass something to someone' = chuyển giao, đưa cái gì cho ai xem/xử lý. • Câu 3: 'pass someone' = vượt qua ai đó (trên đường cao tốc). ⚠️ Bẫy: Cần chú ý chia thì của động từ. Cả ba câu đều diễn tả hành động đã xảy ra trong quá khứ ('had a long wait', 'so that I could check', 'was going far too fast') nên từ cần điền phải ở dạng quá khứ phân từ/quá khứ đơn: PASSED. Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 56 Method: complete the gap with "PASSED" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write one word which fits all three sentences. Q40. • They had a long wait at the airport so they __________ the time.... Correct completion: PASSED.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 4: Gapped Sentences

questions.instruction:
Q41.
• You need to __________ on the right side of Mrs Smith — she can be very difficult if she gets annoyed with you.
• The doctor says I'll need to __________ on taking the tablets for at least a week.
• You can __________ that bicycle for as long as you want — I never use it these days.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: KEEP (động từ: giữ, tiếp tục, giữ lại). • Câu 1: 'keep on the right side of someone' = làm vừa lòng/giữ mối quan hệ tốt đẹp với ai đó để tránh rắc rối. • Câu 2: 'keep on doing something' = tiếp tục làm gì (ở đây là tiếp tục uống thuốc). • Câu 3: 'keep something' = giữ lại cái gì để sử dụng. 💡 Mẹo: 'Keep' là động từ rất thông dụng tạo ra nhiều phrasal verbs phổ biến: keep on (tiếp tục), keep up (duy trì), keep off (tránh xa). Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 56 Method: complete the gap with "KEEP" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure. Language rule: key word transformation keeps the key word unchanged while preserving meaning through grammar pattern and lexical control.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write one word which fits all three sentences. Q41. • You need to __________ on the right side of Mrs Smith — she can.... Correct completion: KEEP.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 4: Gapped Sentences

questions.instruction:
Q42.
• The film examines the theme of what it is like to live in a strange __________ far from home.
• He owned a large amount of __________ in the north of the country, and was said to be extremely rich.
• The __________ in that area is very fertile, and supports a wide variety of crops.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: LAND (danh từ: vùng đất, đất đai/bất động sản, thổ nhưỡng). • Câu 1: 'live in a strange land' = sống ở một xứ sở/vùng đất xa lạ. • Câu 2: 'own a large amount of land' = sở hữu một lượng lớn đất đai/bất động sản. • Câu 3: 'the land is fertile' = đất đai phì nhiêu/màu mỡ. 💡 Mẹo: Phân biệt các nét nghĩa rộng của 'land' từ nghĩa văn chương (xứ sở, quốc gia) cho đến nghĩa thực tế (đất đai thuộc quyền sở hữu) và địa lý (thổ nhưỡng trồng trọt). Source: CAE 3, Test 4, Page 56 Method: complete the gap with "LAND" only after checking the words before and after it. Trap: keep the sentence grammatical and avoid answers that fit the topic but break the structure. Language rule: key word transformation keeps the key word unchanged while preserving meaning through grammar pattern and lexical control.
questions.answerLocation: Source gap: Write one word which fits all three sentences. Q42. • The film examines the theme of what it is like to live in a str.... Correct completion: LAND.
questions.source: Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English 3, Cambridge University Press, Part 4: Gapped Sentences
